---
title: 'Distortion Critique 3'
date: '2016-09-18T04:51:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '110968226823941516'
blogger_filename: '/2016/09/distortion-critique-3.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'critique'
  - 'White Noise'
---

<br />So I put out scene one from Distortion for a writer's critique. Here was the result:<br /><br />It's amazing how awesome this feedback was. You can live with your stuff long enough that you become blind to it. I have a lot of work to do (first novel) but I've studied the craft long enough to understand everything everyone is saying. Besides all the specific points, the two themes I noticed were a lack of emotional/physical reaction (nothing to keep you in Ash's head) and the Great Sin (show don't tell). I also changed tense and didn't notice (no that was not on purpose). There were many other great points, and I have a lot of work to do. Thanks again to&nbsp;<a href="http://www.scribophile.com/" target="_blank">http://www.scribophile.com/&nbsp;</a>for making a community for writers.<br /><br /><div class="user" style="position: relative; z-index: 10;"><div style="font-size: 14px; font-weight: bold; text-align: center; word-wrap: break-word;"><a href="http://www.scribophile.com/authors/shaun-baines/" style="color: #c70c14; outline: 0px; text-decoration: none;">Shaun Baines</a></div></div><div class="bubble" style="border-radius: 8px; border: 2px solid rgb(221, 221, 221); margin-left: 90px; position: relative;"><div class="comment" style="background-color: white; color: #222222; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; padding: 10px;"><h4 style="border-bottom: 2px solid rgb(238, 238, 238); color: #353e34; font-size: 16px; line-height: 22px; margin: 0px 0px 10px; padding-bottom: 5px;">Opening Comments</h4><div style="margin-top: 10px;">Like the title, like the concept.&nbsp; I'll talk about the more technical issues as I go</div><h4 style="border-bottom: 2px solid rgb(238, 238, 238); color: #353e34; font-size: 16px; line-height: 22px; margin: 40px 0px 10px; padding-bottom: 5px;">Inline Critique</h4><div class="inline-comment-body" id="inline-comment-651360"><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important;">Out of the corner of his eye, through the window of The Grounded Cafe, Ash saw two men dressed in black combat gear duck behind a car.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">I like the opening as it immediately places&nbsp;me in Ash's world.&nbsp; I like the name of the café as it quickly becomes apparent that Ash is anything but grounded.&nbsp; I also like how&nbsp;Ash is an Airman back in civilian life and so is 'grounded.'&nbsp; A lot of thought went into the name, which&nbsp;I appreciate more on the second reading.&nbsp; Gives the beginning a&nbsp;surreal&nbsp;edginess.</ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Coolness rushed down his spine, and his muscles tensed.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">I didn't buy this as a description of his response.&nbsp; 'Coolness rushed down his spine' is a bit too prosaic.</ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">He reached for his sidearm.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">He shook his head, and looked again, they were gone.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;"></ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Ash relaxed and sank back into the leather bound chair; alone, in the corner<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">repetition of the word corner - jars me out of the story</ins>of his favorite coffee shop.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Always keep your back to the wall, be near the most exits, but never too visible. It was like breathing, it never stopped... even in civilian life. Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder, PTSD. That was the diagnosis they’d assigned him.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">As Dean of Military Science, Ash's job primarily lay in administrative work and policy these days, plus a few sessions counseling the younger cadets, and would-be trouble makers. He was<br /><del style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #ec6f6f; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">quite</del>&nbsp;overqualified for the position, but he ran a tight unit.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">He stared at the faces of the pedestrians as they passed by the window. Hurried. Busy. Buried in their smartphones. So oblivious to the world around them, or the little clouds that float around their shoulders.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">should be 'floated'.&nbsp; This gives a nice&nbsp;introduction into Ash's issue.&nbsp; It helps to make the rest of the story more believable</ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">The leaves began to fall, most turning shades of orange and yellow, matching perfectly the Pumpkins that were dotting porches and windows through the city the past few weeks.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">This is a great, great description.&nbsp; It tells me exactly what season it is without mentioning it by name.&nbsp; It also gives a good indicator of the type of place&nbsp;Ash inhabits.</ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Some commotion outside broke his train of thought...<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">what train of thought?&nbsp; All he was doing was watching people in the street.&nbsp; he wasn't thinking about anything</ins>&nbsp;Everyone was stopping, turning, and staring in the same direction, a few moving toward the distraction.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">this</ins><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">this sentence needs work. maybe break it up&nbsp;into two.</ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Ash stepped outside to see what was happening, turned a corner, and nearly ran into a bystander.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">what happens to this bystander? why is he there, other than to highlight Ash is distracted.</ins>&nbsp;A brisk evening breeze broke past his collar.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">I like this description.&nbsp; Nice alliteration, but it seems to come from nowhere.&nbsp; Ash doesn't even&nbsp;respond to it.</ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">A man in a tattered and dirty suit with a graying beard was standing on top of a pickup truck screaming at nobody in particular.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">"You always did love Mother best... I know... That's why... You couldn't keep your mouth shut..." On he went, incoherently.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Just as Ash took a step closer,<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">why did he take a step closer?&nbsp; does he know the guy?&nbsp; does he just enjoy watching a freakshow?&nbsp; You need to be clearer&nbsp;in showing the character's motivations</ins>the man turned to stare him directly in the eye.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">In a deeper, gravelly voice: "You... What do you want with us?"</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Ash took a step back.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Then the man turned back to his one-sided conversation.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Ash could see a large creature behind the man. The bottom half was more mist than form, as though it were made of smoke.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">he's either mist or smoke.&nbsp; they're two different things and can't be both</ins>The top half was like a man, with the head of a bull. It was charcoal,<br /><del style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #ec6f6f; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">and</del><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">with</ins>streaks of white painted across the chest.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Ash<br /><del style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #ec6f6f; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">&nbsp;could feel his palms turn cold, and he&nbsp;</del>felt as though he couldn't move his legs.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">I don't think palms ever turn cold.&nbsp; It's an odd description and not needed here</ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">A smaller creature was on the homeless man's shoulders, screaming into his ears.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">I love this bit.&nbsp; It illustrates madness beautifully.&nbsp; It makes me sad for the homeless man while wondering why Ash sees it and no-one else does.</ins>He couldn't hear the conversation, but Ash could tell that the homeless man was responding and that he had only been hearing one-half of the conversation. Ash looked around and nobody else seemed too surprised, as usual.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">The large creature stared directly at Ash, cocked his head to one side and disappeared along with the smaller one. The homeless man was by himself again, then his eyes narrowed. He leapt off the truck and ran at Ash.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">I don't know why the homeless man suddenly decides to charge Ash, especially as the creatures tormenting&nbsp;him have disappeared.&nbsp; Could they talk to the man one last time before disappearing?&nbsp; Seemingly urging him to attack Ash?</ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">It took all his training to keep from being pinned down. The old man was wiry but stronger than he looked.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">Feels like I missed something here.&nbsp; Where's the fighting part?</ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Ash let out a cry, "Jesus!"</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">The wiry man stepped back, his eyes darted around, as though he looked confused.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">That was all Ash needed. In 30 seconds he hog-tied the man with his own belt.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">This</ins><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">This fight is over before&nbsp;it even began.&nbsp; I would give a&nbsp;good description here to add a bit of action.</ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">The police were just arriving. "Charlie's at it again... Let's take him in."<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">I like how he is known to the police.&nbsp; it adds character to the homeless man</ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Three big men in uniform put cuffs and a muzzle on the old man, and threw him, rather harshly, into the back of the squad car.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Ash heard someone behind him, "Those were some moves, Marine."</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">"Airman. Uh... Thanks. No harm done." Ash noticed his cup crushed beneath him, the ice, coffee, and cream covered the sidewalk, and his jeans. "Well... Almost no harm anyway."</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">"Why don't you let me get you another… on me."</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Ash looked up to see a balding man in blue slacks, yellow polo, and a pile of papers in his arms.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">You should describe this man as soon as the conversation begins.&nbsp; It feels like&nbsp;Ash has began a conversation&nbsp;without looking at him.&nbsp; I get that Ash is distracted by his lost cup of coffee, but the exchange feels unnatural&nbsp;without immediate eye contact</ins>&nbsp;A logo with two E’s overlapping bore prominently on his chest.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">this is a nice detail I feel&nbsp;will become important further into the narrative</ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">"The least I could do for a man who'd be willing to step into a situation like that, protect all these people. What's your name son?"</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">"Ash. I need to get some new clothes. I don't think I need any more caffeine right now. But thanks for the offer." With a wave, he set off toward home.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">It feels like you have written off this encounter as nothing important when it clearly is.&nbsp; Perhaps you could add something like&nbsp;'Ash noticed the stranger watching him as he walked away and it made him uncomfortable'.&nbsp; Just something to show the bald man has an interest in him, even though Ash has none in him.</ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Ash headed down the sidewalk, past the shops and restaurants. There is a girl crying,<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">written in present tense, should be past, unless I'm missing something</ins>typing away on the phone. A little red frog on her shoulder was yelling into her ear.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">I like how these creatures don't have to be humanoid.&nbsp; they could be anything.&nbsp; I don't think you need the next sentence.&nbsp; I would think Ash wasn't trying to imagine the other side of the conversation at all</ins>&nbsp;Ash could only imagine what it must be saying.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">A little old lady is sitting inside a window at the nursing home. She is knitting something, probably a blanket. A large man in all white, stands behind her, smiling. He looks up at Ash, his smile fades slightly, and then returns. He looks down to her again.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">If these encounters are&nbsp;written in present tense in order to differentiate them from the rest of the narrative, I don't think it works.&nbsp; It's a bit too distracting.</ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Ash could not see the figure accompanying each person, only certain ones, here and there. Most were just clouds or mist.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">Don't need this explanation here.&nbsp; I am getting the&nbsp;gist of what is going on.&nbsp; I don't need to have it spelled out and ruin the tension</ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">"Don't worry so much." His doctor told him, "These may be after effects of the war. We’re just beginning to understand PTSD. The mind is a marvelous adapter to stress. As long as these phantoms do not interrupt your daily work, or cause you to want to harm yourself or others, you should be fine. Consider yourself lucky. Just think of them as a construct of your active imagination, an amusing distraction from the mundane realities of life."</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">He turned off the street into an alley between two old buildings, and up a well-worn path into the woods, his refuge from the masses.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">If he has walked</ins><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">If he&nbsp;has walked from a town into the woods, you need more description of his surroundings to help convey the passage of time&nbsp;it took to make his journey</ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Ash opened the door of his cabin.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">It wasn't a mansion by any means, but it was more than sufficient for him. 900 sq. ft., one large open room, a loft, and a balcony.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Yes sir, he owned one fine cabin in the woods. All to himself. How he'd managed to live here eight years and still not have a single friend invited over was a mystery, even to himself. Then again, you'd have to have a friend to invite one.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">He liked things just so. He liked the solitude.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">At least that's what he told himself.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Ash took a seat on his balcony, overlooking the hills and lake. He stared at the expansive forests, lost in time. The clock read: 6:00pm, he laid down in his hammock, bundled in the overpriced winter resistant sleeping bag, and watched the stars from the balcony until he drifted off.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">As sleep overtook him, he thought to himself "Yes sir, this is one fine cabin."<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">Nice ending to this bit.&nbsp; As soon as a character&nbsp;expresses contentment, the reader automatically assumes that will be disrupted.&nbsp; It's exciting</ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">***</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">An explosion knocks him against a wall. He barely sees,<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">should this comma be here?</ins>&nbsp;through the smoke, troops running about, hauling rubble off<br /><del style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #ec6f6f; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">of</del>&nbsp;bodies, checking pulses. Some are screaming in pain. Others shouting to one another. Half the mess hall is completely gone, all that is left is fire and rubble.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">I think if this is a stream of consciousness type dream, the punctuation should either all be commas or all full stops.&nbsp; Mixing the two confuses things</ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Ash can feel the beat of his heart in his neck,<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">I like this description</ins>&nbsp;his forehead rushed with sweat.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">do foreheads get rushed with sweat?</ins>He sees them all running around, but all he hears is intense ringing.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">repetition of the word 'all'</ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">As the numbness subsides from his brain,<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">judging&nbsp;by the writing, I'm surprised Ash feels numb. My heart is racing!</ins>shock waves hit his chest as more buildings are getting hit nearby.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Ash uses the wall to steady himself and works to stand on his feet.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Others will tend to the wounded. This band of Jihadist miscreants<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">this is just a personal thing, but I've never thought of Jihadists as miscreants?&nbsp; I would use that word to describe teenage tearaways&nbsp;not extremists.</ins>&nbsp;found upgraded weapons; Russian no doubt.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Ash shoots out the building, down the road to the artillery unit. He grabs his rifle and heads for the highest structure, a radio tower toward the side of the camp.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">He can see the launch area<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">from</ins>where the rockets are being fired<br /><del style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #ec6f6f; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">from</del>. He takes a few quick breaths, hyperventilating floods the lungs with oxygen<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">this sentence doesn't make sense to me</ins>, one last deep breath in and hold, steadies his body.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Through the scope, he can see about 10 of them.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">are there ten or about ten?&nbsp; It only matters because you are so detailed when it comes to counting them down each time Ash kills one of them.</ins></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">They only have two missile launchers. They have very little cover, firing from open desert, mostly hiding behind their Jeep and a few large outcroppings of fallen mountainside.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">He fires two rounds back to back, taking out both missile launchers, one exploding in the handlers face. Ash then begins firing one round after another as the attackers fall like Coke cans on a fence post.<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">like this image</ins>The rounds hit their targets with extreme prejudice.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Three remain, cowering behind the Jeep. Jeeps<br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">repetition of jeep</ins>&nbsp;are great vehicles for driving through tough terrain quickly, but they are built light, not armored. He sees one peek through the glass, gone.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">The other two attempt to climb in the vehicle keeping their heads down and drive away. The problem with that is that both seats are occupied. He doesn't need to see them. Two. One.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">The Jeep speeds up as it drives erratically, then crashes into a mound of rocks.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">***</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Ash woke up... another nightmare, if you can call a memory a nightmare.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">He rubbed his eyes and glanced at his wrist.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Red numbers flooded his vision, 3:00 am.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">He rolled over and went back to sleep.</div></div><h4 style="border-bottom: 2px solid rgb(238, 238, 238); color: #353e34; font-size: 16px; line-height: 22px; margin: 40px 0px 10px; padding-bottom: 5px;">Closing Comments</h4><div style="margin-top: 10px;">There are lots of things to like about this with a lot of scope for you to explore.&nbsp; I like how Ash is an ordinary man with extraordinary things going on around him.&nbsp; There is also an interesting question over whether he is suffering PTSD or there is something larger at stake.</div><div style="margin-top: 10px;">I think there are a few things you might like to improve upon.&nbsp; I like the short paragraphs as it adds pace, but without longer, more descriptive passages it lacks depth.&nbsp; Swapping and changing between the two keeps the reader engaged.&nbsp;</div><div style="margin-top: 10px;">There is no physical description of Ash, which makes him difficult to identify with.&nbsp; He is coming across as a loner, which is fine, but he also feels cold and indifferent.&nbsp; He hog ties a troubled homeless man without thinking twice.&nbsp; In his dream, he shoots other human beings as if they were nothing more than Coke cans on a fence.&nbsp; He wakes from his nightmare and goes back to sleep!&nbsp; It's hard to engage with a character like that unless you imbue him with a few human characteristics.</div><div style="margin-top: 10px;">The dream sequence starts well - all short sentences and action, but toward the end, it becomes more poetic and descriptive.&nbsp; I think doing it either way is fine (though I prefer the short, choppy sentences), but using both techniques alters the tone of the writing halfway through and is a bit jarring.</div><div style="margin-top: 10px;">The one thing I can't understand is the end of your chapter and how it works so well.&nbsp; Basically, he goes to sleep.&nbsp; Everything I know about writing tells me there should be a hook or a tease to make the reader want to turn the page.&nbsp; You've somehow done that without&nbsp;using that tired tradition.&nbsp; I genuinely do not know how you did it, but&nbsp;the ending to&nbsp;Chapter 1 makes me want to read Chapter 2!</div><div style="margin-top: 10px;">Thanks for letting me read this.&nbsp; if you think I've been harsh or useless, I'm sorry.&nbsp; I&nbsp;only offer my opinion in the hope it will help.</div><div style="margin-top: 10px;">Good luck&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div></div></div><blockquote class="tr_bq" style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: Verdana, Geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 15.4px; line-height: 19.6px;"><div><br /></div></blockquote><h4 style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: Verdana, Geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 15.4px; line-height: 19.6px; margin: 0px; position: relative;">By&nbsp;<a href="https://plus.google.com/+DarrellWolfe/posts" style="color: #149adf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe</a></h4><blockquote class="tr_bq" style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: Verdana, Geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 15.4px; line-height: 19.6px;"><i>Storyteller | Creative | <a href="http://www.16personalities.com/infj-personality" style="color: #149adf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">INFJ</a>&nbsp;|&nbsp;<a href="https://www.gallupstrengthscenter.com/Purchase/en-US/Product?Path=Clifton%20StrengthsFinder" style="color: #149adf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">Intellection</a>&nbsp;|&nbsp;Ideation | Input | Learner | Achiever | Multipotentialite</i></blockquote><ul><li><i>See my full about page and disclosures: <a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/p/what-is-this.html" target="_blank">Click Here</a>.&nbsp;</i></li><li><i>Also check out my <a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/p/affiliate-store.html" target="_blank">Resources and Suggestions </a>for writers (<a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/p/affiliate-store.html" target="_blank">here</a>).&nbsp;</i></li><li><i>To Subscribe to the <a href="http://feeds.feedburner.com/AuthorFun" target="_blank">RSS: Click Here</a></i></li><li><i>For Non-Fiction writing by <a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe: Click Here</a></i></li></ul><ul></ul>
